title: A Doctor Who Loves Vaccines AND Healthy Food Might Be America's Top Doc!
summary: Imagine a doctor who tells you to eat less junk food AND get your shots. That's Dr. Nicole Saphier, and she might become America's next surgeon general—the nation's top doctor! President Trump picked her after his first choice, Dr. Casey Means, couldn't get enough votes. Dr. Saphier is a radiologist, which means she's an expert at reading X-rays and scans. She works at a famous cancer center and has spent years talking about health on Fox News. She's a big fan of the 'Make America Healthy Again' plan, which wants to remove weird chemicals from food and get kids moving. But here's the twist: she's way more pro-vaccine than some other people in the government. She even praised the CDC for telling people to get measles shots, saying 'the more vaccine confusion we create, the more preventable disease we will see.' She's not afraid to speak up. Last year, she called a government health report 'pretty embarrassing' because it had mistakes. She also said firing a top health official was 'a mess.' Dr. Saphier is a mom of three boys and had her first son when she was just 17. She says that experience made her want to help other young moms. If she gets the job, she'll be able to warn Americans about health dangers—like a coach giving a pep talk for the whole country!
why_it_matters: If Dr. Saphier becomes surgeon general, she could help make school lunches healthier and keep your community safer from diseases.

keywords.explanation: A doctor who specializes in reading X-rays and scans.
The top doctor in the United States who gives health advice to the whole country.
Being in favor of vaccines to protect against diseases.
The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention, a U.S. agency that fights diseases.
An illness that can be stopped from happening, often through vaccines.
A very contagious disease that causes a rash and fever, and can be prevented by a vaccine.

questions.question: Who is Dr. Nicole Saphier?
options: A chef who cooks healthy food
A radiologist who might be the next surgeon general
The president's first choice for surgeon general
questions.correct_answer: A radiologist who might be the next surgeon general

questions.question: Why did President Trump pick Dr. Saphier?
options: Dr. Casey Means couldn't get enough votes
Dr. Saphier was his first choice
Dr. Saphier is a famous singer
questions.correct_answer: Dr. Casey Means couldn't get enough votes

questions.question: What is the 'Make America Healthy Again' plan about?
options: Removing chemicals from food and encouraging kids to move
questions.correct_answer: Removing chemicals from food and encouraging kids to move

questions.question: How does Dr. Saphier feel about vaccines?
What did Dr. Saphier say about a government health report?
options: It was the best report ever
It was 'pretty embarrassing' due to mistakes
questions.correct_answer: It was 'pretty embarrassing' due to mistakes

questions.question: How old was Dr. Saphier when she had her first son?

background_read: The surgeon general job was created in 1871 to give the country's top health advice.
President Donald Trump (who became the U.S. president again in 2025) has picked a new one.
Measles outbreaks have happened recently because some people stopped vaccinating.
Article_Structure: WHO: Dr. Nicole Saphier, a radiologist and mom of three boys.
WHAT: She might become the next surgeon general, the nation's top doctor.
WHERE: United States, where she works at a cancer center and talks about health on TV.
WHY: President Trump chose her after his first pick couldn't get enough votes, and she supports vaccines and healthier food.

perspectives.description: Supporters say she'll keep kids healthy by promoting vaccines and good food.
Some critics think she's too friendly with TV networks instead of focusing only on science.
Many see her as a mom who understands the struggles of young parents.
